result:
The fix worked — we got past the original assertion. The test now hits a different (separate) stub.

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:454` had a `TODO(port)` that completely elided the hook call. Combined with `crates/lua-stdlib/src/debug_lib.rs:776` passing `None` instead of the `hookf` trampoline to `set_hook_full`, no debug hooks ever fired. `locals.lua:574` (`checktable(trace, {"return sethook", "return close", ...})`) failed because `trace` stayed empty.

**Fix:**
1. `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs` — `do_::hook` now builds a `LuaDebug` with the event/line and actually invokes `state.hook` using the standard take/call/restore pattern (so the borrow of `&mut LuaState` is reusable inside the callback).
2. `crates/lua-stdlib/src/debug_lib.rs` — `hookf` now takes `(event: i32, currentline: i32)` directly (the lua-stdlib `DebugInfo` and the canonical `lua_vm::debug::LuaDebug` are distinct types). It uses the real event to index `HOOKNAMES`, fixing the always-`"call"` bug.
3. `db_sethook` no longer passes `None` to `set_hook_full`; instead it installs a closure wrapper (via `lua_vm::debug::set_hook`) that forwards `ar.event` / `ar.currentline` to `hookf`.

**New state:** Build green. The original `stdin:583: assertion failed!` is gone. Test now fails downstream on a separate `phase-b: trace_call` Phase-B stub at `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:2083` — a different bug the mega-loop will retarget.

tool_input.command: cargo build -p lua-cli 2>&1 | grep -E "^(error|warning: unused.*unused)" | head -10; echo "exit=$?"
